race_name: Tour of the Battenkill — Gran Fondo

top_categories.workout_context: When someone attacks on mixed-surface — paved roads and packed dirt farm, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Tour of the Battenkill — Gran Fondo's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
70ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
64 miles on mixed-surface — paved roads and packed dirt farm burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Tour of the Battenkill — Gran Fondo. Train it until it feels boring.
Tour of the Battenkill — Gran Fondo's mixed-surface — paved roads and packed dirt farm forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 64 miles.
At Tour of the Battenkill — Gran Fondo, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed-surface — paved roads and packed dirt farm. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 4,500ft of climbing on mixed-surface — paved roads and packed dirt farm does to your power file on race day.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Tour of the Battenkill — Gran Fondo, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Base fitness for Tour of the Battenkill — Gran Fondo. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Tour of the Battenkill — Gran Fondo’s 64 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 64 miles of mixed-surface — paved roads and packed dirt farm roads in Cambridge, Washington County, New York, USA.
